title: annotate
description: Adds, updates, or clears annotations that stay on screen until something clears them. Unlike a screenshot's own `annotations` — which live only for that capture — these are drawn into the page, so they appear in recordings and in any screenshot taken while they're up. Annotations added here persist across steps and across navigation until an `annotate` step clears them, their `duration` elapses, or the context ends.
$comment: Structured as allOf [shape, guard] with both halves written INLINE rather than $ref'd into components: the dereferencer inlines them into the root anyway AND keeps the components copy, which would double this schema's size in every consumer (it already carries two copies of annotation_v3, one for `add` and one for `update`).

add.description: Annotations to draw. Give an annotation an `id` to update or clear it later.

update.description: Changes to annotations that are already on screen, matched by `id`. Each entry replaces the annotation with that id.

items.title: Identified annotation
items.description: An annotation that names the `id` of the annotation to replace.

allOf.title: id is required for updates
allOf.$comment: `update` addresses an annotation that's already on screen; without an id there's nothing to address, so this is rejected at validation time rather than becoming a silent no-op.

clear.description: Annotations to remove. `true` clears every annotation; a list of ids clears just those.

anyOf.title: Clear all
anyOf.description: If `true`, clears every annotation currently on screen. If `false`, clears nothing.

anyOf.title: Clear by id
anyOf.description: Ids of the annotations to clear. Ids that aren't on screen are ignored.

allOf.title: At least one of add, update, or clear
allOf.$comment: An annotate step that does none of the three is a no-op the author didn't mean to write.
